prose:
**Acrolein Fukui reactivity analysis** — DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (tier `standard`, density-fitting RIJK) in PySCF via chemkit, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/acrolein.xyz` used as-is (no optimization) for a single-point Fukui analysis (`DFT//user geometry`). Mulliken charges (PySCF) were computed for the neutral (charge 0, multiplicity 1), the cation (charge +1, multiplicity 2), and the anion (charge −1, multiplicity 2) states. SCF converged in each case. Integrity checks reported the following warnings verbatim: 

• sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• common header present

From the computed Fukui indices (Mulliken scheme), the most electrophilic site is atom 4 (C) with f⁺ = 0.64232102 e, and the most nucleophilic site is atom 1 (O) with f⁻ = 0.39388616 e. Files: JSON output at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpmcnb6cek/live.json`, Fukui plot at `/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/acrolein/argo_o4-mini/20260703-185617_acrolein_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png`. CLI: `cli.py fukui --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 acrolein.xyz --out live.json`.
